body:
## What

Six observe-only Python pieces that advance the AI_OS self-build loop toward vacation mode. All additive, DRY_RUN, no commit/push/merge/execute. Each: build → pytest → dogfood. **Not to be merged by Claude.**

### Pieces
1. **`aios_self_build_evidence_ledger.py`** — reads `Reports/self_build_cycle/*.json` back into one human-reviewable digest (decision/safety/human-required distribution, latest cycle, posture). Aggregate-only: never copies `evidence_bundle` forward, so no value can leak through.
2. **`aios_decision_to_packet_drafter.py`** — goal candidate → governed `CODEX-ONLY PROMPT` packet **DRAFT** (all required sections, DRY_RUN, full forbidden-paths, per-action approval + STOP). Writes only under `Reports/self_build_drafts/`; **hard-refuses `work_packets/`**.
3. **`aios_packet_completeness_review.py`** — draft → `READY_FOR_HUMAN_REVIEW | INCOMPLETE | PROMOTION_BLOCKED` via governance shape + readiness checks. Decides readiness for human review, never APPLY.
4. **`aios_approval_review_compressor.py`** — packet + governance verdict + completeness verdict + risk → ONE compact approval card with a recommended decision **label**. `requires_human` always True; approves nothing.
5. **`aios_apply_counterpart_inventory.py`** — read-only scan mapping every `*.DRY_RUN.*` script to whether an APPLY twin exists, ranked. Builds the inventory; **creates no APPLY file**.
6. **`aios_path_guard_validator.py`** — changed files vs allowed/forbidden paths → PASS/BLOCK, callable from a pre-commit hook so the allowed-path rule becomes **enforced code**, not docs.

## Validation
- **46 tests pass** across the six modules.
- **End-to-end dogfood**: classifier gap → draft → governance (`WARN`, 0 errors) → completeness (`READY`) → approval card (`READY_FOR_HUMAN_APPROVAL`, risk `LOW`, `requires_human=True`) → path-guard (`PASS`). Every stage observe-only with the human gate intact.
- **APPLY inventory against the real repo**: 343 DRY_RUN scripts, 88 mutation, only 3 with APPLY twins → **85 HIGH-priority gaps** (the precise execute-half backlog).

## Scope note (why this is "Lane A")
This is the cloud-buildable, observe-only half of loop closure. The remaining work is hard-gated by AI_OS's own policy and belongs to other lanes: relay processor + APPLY counterparts + soak run = Codex East (local pwsh); real SOS channel + scheduler registration = Human Owner only. Liveness watchdog (`aios_deadman_watchdog.py`) and enforcement hooks/CI already exist on main.

Pre-existing PowerShell tests in `tests/orchestration` still fail in the no-`pwsh` cloud container — identical to main; verify on CI / Codex East.
title: feat(autonomy): loop-closure Lane A — evidence ledger + drafter, promotion review, approval card, APPLY inventory, path-guard

body:
## Summary

The **autonomy-completion Big-Pack** — one governed packet that sequences the **5 remaining themes** into gated modules for **Codex East (local)**. From the what's-left sweep on `main` (`56019dc5`): the autonomy *parts* are merged (control plane #510, router, discovery, autonomy_loop, packet_runner) — but **nothing is connected, armed, or proven.** `aios_governance_validator` → **PASS, 0 warnings.**

## Why a prompt, not me building it
I can't safely execute these from here: **no `pwsh`** in my container, CONNECT edits the **protected night cycle next to your live lane**, and ARM/PROVE need **your machine + credentials + a real run**. Codex East on `C:\Dev\Ai.Os` is the right executor (it has all of that).

## The 5 themes (gated, reuse-not-duplicate)
| Theme | What | Owner |
|---|---|---|
| **1 CONNECT** 🔑 | observe-only night-cycle phase runs control-plane + spine each cycle → the loop **drives the brain**; connect the 7 zero-caller components | **this packet** |
| **2 TRUST** | wire completion-evidence validator into outcome classification | references **#509** |
| **3 ACT** | self-heal real `-Apply` (bounded/gated) + improvement/execution preview→goal | dispatcher→assign deferred to **T2B** |
| **4 ARM** | design-only: SOS deferred to **#502**; scheduler = final Human-Owner step, **not activated** | references **#502** |
| **5 PROVE** | readiness checklist for first real run | references **#472**; run is yours, local |

## Safety
DRY_RUN-first · per-theme APPLY gate · **CONNECT phase is observe-only** (mutates no queue/lock/approval/dispatch) · **no scheduler/SOS/real-run activation here** · reuse merged parts (no parallel brain) · collision-check + **STOP on your local lane** · no secrets/broker/live.

**The keystone is CONNECT** — it turns all your merged parts into one running system. Everything else either references an existing owner or is design-only.

Merging queues the packet. Each theme's APPLY needs your separate explicit approval. Human Owner approval final.

body:
## Summary

Closes **blocker B7** (multi-day resource accumulation) from the vacation-mode plan — **while preserving proof history as the memory spine for Module 5.** Doc-only governed proposal. `aios_governance_validator` → **PASS, 0 warnings.**

## Recon (duplicate-prevention)
- `automation/orchestration/hygiene/Rotate-AiOsLogs.ps1` = canonical rotation owner (size + age + gzip + delete) **but filters `*.log` only**.
- **No `.jsonl`-rotation owner** and **no general retention engine** exist (only `Clear-AiOsStaleApprovals.ps1` + retention *drafts*).
- Unbounded `.jsonl` ledgers: work ledger, cost ledger, supervisor-cycle ledger, productivity, forex journals.

## Two gated modules
- **M1 — extend the canonical owner:** add `.jsonl` rotation to `Rotate-AiOsLogs.ps1` via an **opt-in switch** (default `*.log` behavior unchanged). Ledgers are **rotated + gzipped — history preserved**, never hard-deleted inside the keep horizon.
- **M2 — bounded retention engine (new):** scans accumulating `telemetry/`, `relay/`, `Reports/` dirs; **DRY_RUN reports candidates and deletes nothing**; APPLY **compresses before deleting**, and only deletes a compressed artifact past a long explicit retention horizon.

## The "preserve proof history" guarantee (your reason for prioritizing this)
A **hard keep-list never pruned:** proof/evidence, soak evidence, approval records, governance records, the cycle marker, source authority. Fails closed when a target is uncertain. **No mass delete** — every action per-file, bounded, logged. The approvals-cleanup lane stays owned by `Clear-AiOsStaleApprovals` (not duplicated).

## Why before Module 5
`.jsonl` rotation + retention = Module 5's clean logs / replay / audit history. This lands first so the factory has a durable memory spine.

DRY_RUN-first, per-module APPLY gates, your separate explicit approval each. Human Owner approval final.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LbY23a3mfrpTFXp6GzfoSD

body:
## Summary

The **single current owner** for SOS last-mile arming — the #1 silent-death blocker for vacation-mode proof. Recon confirmed **no SOS packet exists on `main`** and the 3 SOS branches (`sos-only-alert-rule`, `sos-display-vs-wake`, `sos-telegram-tasker-scaffold`) are **stale, 0 commits ahead**. This **supersedes the stale #468**.

Doc-only governed proposal. `aios_governance_validator` → **PASS, 0 warnings.**

## ⚠️ Elevated risk — NOT additive
Unlike the spine modules, this **edits `notifier.py` + the protected night-cycle notifier call and lifts the Telegram safety block.** So it's structured as **two separately-gated phases**:
- **Phase A — arm the notifier:** reconcile env-var names (`AIOS_TG_*`), lift the Telegram block *for Telegram only*, **loud-fail on missing credential** (no silent file-fallback), preserve quiet-by-default (only `BLOCKED` wakes). + e2e synthetic-BLOCKED test.
- **Phase B — route the loop:** minimal night-cycle change so a `BLOCKED` routes through the armed channel when enabled.

## Safety (encoded)
- **DRY_RUN-first**, separate APPLY approval per phase.
- **Operator supplies the credential** via env/untracked config — **no token/chat-id/secret ever committed** (validator asserts this).
- Live path activates **only** when the existing **`relay/approvals/enable-sos-notifier.approval.md` gate (STATUS: WAITING)** is moved to approved **and** the credential is present — wires into the existing gate, doesn't invent one.
- No email/push/ADB/broker/webhook/scheduler/dashboard. Wake-severity model not widened.

## Why now
Closes blocker **B4** from the vacation-mode 10-step plan. With heartbeat (you, local), restart supervisor (merged), and soak harness (merged) in place, the armed SOS channel is what lets a 2–4h supervised soak prove "a dead loop pages me."

Merging queues the packet. Each phase's APPLY needs your separate explicit approval. Human Owner approval final.

body:
## Summary

Completes the **unfinished items B + C** of the merged authority packet `AIOS-ENDURANCE-T1-WEEKEND-CRASH-SURVIVAL` (#456). PR #458 delivered item A (heartbeat) as minimum scope but left these two. Pairs with **Packet 2 (SOS arming)** to close the **semi-autonomous weekend** milestone.

Doc-only governed proposal. `aios_governance_validator` → **PASS, 0 warnings.**

## What it specs (DRY_RUN-first, STOP-before-APPLY)
- **Item B — Armed restart supervisor:** a manually-launched loop that runs the deadman watchdog and, on `BLOCKED`, **restarts the night-cycle driver** (which auto-resumes from the marker). DRY_RUN logs `WOULD_RESTART`; **opt-in `-ArmRestart`** to actually restart. STOP-aware, exponential backoff, max-restart cap then stop-and-alert. **No scheduler.** Reuses the existing watchdog + supervisor patterns.
- **Item C — Bounded git/network timeouts:** one reusable helper applied to the ~10 untimed git subprocess calls in `night_supervisor_harness.py` and other hot-path git calls, so a hung call can't wedge the loop.
- **Plus:** per-cycle try/catch error boundary + wire `Reclaim-AiOsOrphans` to sweep `relay/running/`.
- Tests: supervisor logs `WOULD_RESTART` in DRY_RUN; timeout helper returns non-zero on a hung command; error boundary keeps the loop alive.

## Why this + Packet 2 = semi-weekend done
Heartbeat/crash-resume/fail-closed are already live. This adds the **restart actor** (B) + **hang protection** (C); Packet 2 adds the **wake/alert** (SOS). Together: a loop that dies or hangs over a weekend is detected, restarted, and — if it can't recover — pages you.

## Sequence
1 ✅ → 3 (#466) → **B+C (this) + 2** → 4. Gated planning only; APPLY needs your explicit approval.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LbY23a3mfrpTFXp6GzfoSD

body:
## Summary

Adds the local AI_OS Operator Relief Closed Loop v1 and follow-up ADB SOS notification gate/runtime evidence hygiene fixes.

## What changed

- Added a one-shot local operator relief loop that captures repo state, classifies blockers, writes evidence, creates approval-needed items, and exits.
- Added safe validator routing for Python, JSON, Markdown, and PowerShell review signals.
- Added non-executable packet draft generation with resolved branch context.
- Added notification gating that only notifies when Anthony is needed.
- Added optional Android ADB SOS wake rail integration via tools/android/Send-AiosAdbSosWake.ps1.
- Added gitignore rules for Operator Relief runtime evidence while preserving .gitkeep placeholders.
- Added operator-relief tests and workflow documentation.

## Validation

- python -m pytest tests/operator_relief: 16 passed.
- python -m automation.operator_relief.run_operator_relief_loop --send-adb-sos: passed; routine success captured as evidence; no notification/SOS sent because no human action was needed.
- git status --short: clean before push.

## Safety boundaries

- No live trading.
- No broker/API/order execution.
- No secrets or credential loading.
- No Telegram dependency.
- No auto-commit or auto-push inside the loop.
- No persistent watcher or background daemon.
- No governance mutation.

## Copy/paste burden reduced

- Captures repo state, dirty status, evidence path, classification, and next safe action automatically.
- Reduces manual transfer of validator output and packet context.
- Writes approval-needed records locally instead of requiring Anthony to manually summarize them.

## ADB SOS note

ADB SOS is opt-in with --send-adb-sos and only runs when the notification gate determines human attention is required. Clean routine success does not send SOS. The existing Android script remains unchanged.

## No push beyond this PR branch

Only feature/full-operator-relief-closed-loop-v1 was pushed. No direct main push was performed.

## No live trading / broker / secrets

This PR does not add broker execution, API trading, live order paths, secrets, or credential handling.
title: feat: add operator relief closed loop
